info.name: TaskFlow API
info.description:
Workshop API collection for the TaskFlow backend (Spring Boot).

## Quick start
1. Import this file into Postman (File â†’ Import).
2. The `baseUrl` variable defaults to `http://localhost:8080` (local Docker).
   Change it to your Railway URL when testing a deployed backend.
3. Send **Create Task** first â€“ the response `id` is automatically saved to
   the `taskId` variable so GET / PUT / DELETE requests work immediately.

## Endpoints
| Method | Path | Checkpoint |
|--------|------|------------|
| GET | /health | â€“ |
| GET | /actuator/health | â€“ |
| GET | /api/tasks | CP 3 |
| GET | /api/tasks/:id | CP 3 |
| POST | /api/tasks | CP 3 |
| PUT | /api/tasks/:id | CP 3 |
| DELETE | /api/tasks/:id | CP 3 |

variable.description: Backend base URL. Change to your Railway URL when testing a deployed backend.
Auto-populated by the 'Create Task' request. Override manually if needed.

item.name: Health
item.description: Verify the backend is running before testing the Task API.

request.description:
GET /health

Returns a simple JSON payload confirming the backend is up.

Expected response (200 OK):
```json
{
  "status": "UP",
  "service": "taskflow-backend",
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00"
}
```

item.name: Tasks (Checkpoint 3)
item.description:
CRUD endpoints implemented in Checkpoint 3.

Suggested order: Create â†’ Get All â†’ Get by ID â†’ Update â†’ Delete.

request.description:
GET /api/tasks

Returns every task in the database as a JSON array.

Expected response (200 OK):
```json
[
  {
    "id": 1,
    "title": "Buy groceries",
    "description": "Milk, eggs, bread",
    "status": "TODO",
    "createdAt": "2024-01-15T10:30:00"
  }
]
```
Returns an empty array `[]` when no tasks exist.

exec: // Auto-save the new task's id so the GET / PUT / DELETE
// requests below work without any manual copy-paste.

request.description:
POST /api/tasks

Creates a new task. The `id` and `createdAt` fields are generated by the backend.

Required fields:
- `title` (string)

Optional fields:
- `description` (string)
- `status` ("TODO" | "IN_PROGRESS" | "DONE") â€“ defaults to TODO

Expected response (201 Created):
```json
{
  "id": 1,
  "title": "Buy groceries",
  "description": "Milk, eggs, bread",
  "status": "TODO",
  "createdAt": "2024-01-15T10:30:00"
}
```

âšˇ The Tests script automatically saves `id` to the `taskId` collection variable.

GET /api/tasks/:id

Fetches a single task by its ID.

Expected responses:
- 200 OK â€“ task JSON body
- 404 Not Found â€“ when no task exists with that ID

The `{{taskId}}` variable is auto-filled by the Create Task request.

PUT /api/tasks/:id

Replaces all editable fields of an existing task.

Valid status values: `"TODO"` | `"IN_PROGRESS"` | `"DONE"`

Expected responses:
- 200 OK â€“ updated task JSON body
- 404 Not Found â€“ when no task exists with that ID

PUT /api/tasks/:id

Convenience request pre-filled with status DONE.
Useful during Checkpoint 3 to verify status transitions.

DELETE /api/tasks/:id

Permanently removes a task from the database.

Expected responses:
- 204 No Content â€“ task deleted (no response body)
- 404 Not Found â€“ when no task exists with that ID

After deleting, call 'Get all tasks' to confirm the list is updated.
